title: Replace NVIDIA Apex (apex_C) with NPU-native alternatives
symptom: ModuleNotFoundError: cannot import name 'apex_C' (or 'fused_layer_norm', 'FusedAdam', 'FusedSGD', etc.) — NVIDIA Apex library is imported but has no Ascend NPU build or pip package available.
root_cause: NVIDIA Apex is a CUDA-only extension library compiled specifically for NVIDIA GPU architectures. Its C-library (apex_C) and fused operators (fused_layer_norm, FusedAdam, FusedSGD, FusedLAMB, etc.) have no Ascend NPU equivalent. No third-party apex package or NPI variant exists for NPU. The entire Apex dependency must be removed and replaced with NPU-native PyTorch APIs.
fix_steps: 1. Search all Python source files for Apex-related imports: `grep -rn 'from apex' --include='*.py' .` and `grep -rn 'from apex_C' --include='*.py' .`. Also check for `import apex` and any reference to `apex.` in code.
2. Remove ALL `from apex import ...`, `from apex_C import ...`, and `import apex` lines. Do NOT attempt to conditionally import or wrap Apex — it cannot work on NPU.

3. If `apex_C.fused_layer_norm` or `apex.normalization.FusedLayerNorm` was used, replace with `torch.nn.LayerNorm(normalized_shape, eps=1e-5)`. NPU-native LayerNorm provides equivalent semantics. Verify the `normalized_shape` parameter matches the last dimension of the input tensor.

4. If Apex AMP (Automatic Mixed Precision) was used (`from apex import amp`, `amp.initialize()`, `amp.scale_loss()`), replace with PyTorch native autocast: `with torch.autocast(device_type='npu', dtype=torch.float16):` or `with torch.npu.amp.autocast():`. Replace `amp.scale_loss(loss, optimizer)` with `scaler.scale(loss).backward()`, `scaler.step(optimizer)`, `scaler.update()` using `torch.npu.amp.GradScaler()`.

5. If Apex fused optimizers were used (`apex.optimizers.FusedAdam`, `FusedSGD`, `FusedLAMB`, `FusedNovoGrad`), replace with standard PyTorch equivalents: `torch.optim.Adam`, `torch.optim.SGD`, etc. Pass identical learning rate, weight_decay, eps, and betas parameters. Note: Fused optimizers may have marginally different performance but produce functionally equivalent results.
6. If Apex DistributedDataParallel (`apex.parallel.DistributedDDP`) was used, replace with `torch.nn.parallel.DistributedDataParallel`.
7. Verify no remaining references to `apex`, `apex_C`, `amp` (Apex variant), or `Fused*` classes remain in any Python file. Run: `grep -rn 'apex' --include='*.py' .` to confirm.
8. Verify output tensor shapes and dtypes match the original implementation by comparing forward pass outputs with a small test input batch.
affected_patterns: from apex.optimizers import FusedAdam, FusedSGD, FusedLAMB
from apex.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as ApexDDP

code_changes.before:
from apex.normalization import FusedLayerNorm

layer_norm = FusedLayerNorm(normalized_shape=config.hidden_size, eps=1e-5)
code_changes.after:
import torch.nn as nn

layer_norm = nn.LayerNorm(normalized_shape=config.hidden_size, eps=1e-5)

code_changes.file: optimizer.py (or wherever Apex optimizer is instantiated)
code_changes.before:
from apex.optimizers import FusedAdam

optimizer = FusedAdam(model.parameters(), lr=lr, eps=1e-8, weight_decay=weight_decay)
code_changes.after:
import torch.optim as optim

optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=lr, eps=1e-8, weight_decay=weight_decay)

code_changes.file: train.py (or wherever Apex AMP is used)
code_changes.before:
from apex import amp

model, optimizer = amp.initialize(model, optimizer, opt_level='O1')

with amp.scale_loss(loss, optimizer) as scaled_loss:
    scaled_loss.backward()

optimizer.step()
code_changes.after:
scaler = torch.npu.amp.GradScaler()

# Forward pass
with torch.autocast(device_type='npu', dtype=torch.float16):
    output = model(input_ids)
    loss = criterion(output, labels)

# Backward pass
scaler.scale(loss).backward()
scaler.step(optimizer)
scaler.update()

code_changes.file: distributed_setup.py (or wherever Apex DDP is used)
code_changes.before:
from apex.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as ApexDDP

model = ApexDDP(model)
code_changes.after:
from torch.nn.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as DDP

model = DDP(model, device_ids=[local_rank], output_device=local_rank)

antipatterns: Do NOT attempt to install apex via pip or build from source — it will fail with CUDA compilation errors on NPU.
Do NOT attempt to use a third-party 'apex-npu' or 'apex-ascend' package — no such package exists.
Do NOT use CPU fallback for LayerNorm or optimizers — it will OOM on large models.
Do NOT wrap Apex imports in try/except to suppress errors — the code will silently fail at runtime.
Do NOT attempt to compile Apex C++ extensions manually for NPU — the kernel implementations are GPU-specific (CUDA PTX) and have no NPU (BIC/AICORE) equivalent.

meta.refiner_reasoning: This candidate addresses a fundamental dependency incompatibility. The fix is deterministic — Apex must be removed entirely and replaced with NPU-native PyTorch APIs. Multiple sub-patterns exist within Apex (AMP, fused layer norm, fused optimizers, DDP), so all are documented for completeness. The fix generalizes to any CUDA project that depends on NVIDIA Apex for performance optimizations.

problem_description: CUDA projects using NVIDIA Apex library (e.g. `from apex_C import fused_layer_norm` or `from apex import amp, optimizers`) fail immediately on NPU with `ModuleNotFoundError` because the Apex CUDA extension has no Ascend NPI build or equivalent pip package. The `apex_C` C-library is compiled for NVIDIA GPU architecture only. This is NOT a simple .cuda() to .npu() pattern swap — it is an entire third-party library that must be removed and replaced with NPU-native alternatives.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Identify all Apex imports in the project (`apex`, `apex_C`, `fused_layer_norm`, `FusedAdam`, `FusedSGD`, etc.) using grep across source files. 2. Remove all `from apex import ...` and `from apex_C import ...` import lines. 3. Replace `FusedLayerNorm` usage with `torch.nn.LayerNorm` (NPU-native, equivalent semantics). 4. Replace Apex AMP usage with `torch.nn.amp.autocast(device_type="npu")` or `torch.npu.amp.autocast()`. 5. Replace Apex optimizers with standard PyTorch equivalents (`torch.optim.Adam`, etc.). 6. Do NOT attempt to install a third-party apex package — no NPI variant exists. Do NOT use CPU fallback — it will OOM on large models.
name: apex-cuda-extension-removal
